sections.title: A: Write one word in each gap
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: In some cultures, when you are invited to (1) ______ a meal at someone's house, you might be considered rude if you don't say how nice the food is.
In Britain, for example, it's normal for someone to compliment the cook (2) ______ the tastiness of the meal.
You can say something like, 'That was delicious. Do, please, write the recipe down (3) ______ me!'
In other cultures, however, people tend not (4) ______ be so full (5) ______ enthusiasm for the meal.
You might be regarded (6) ______ being rude, as the cook might associate your praise (7) ______ surprise.
He or she might think, 'So, they're shocked I can cook well, are they?'
If you're not sure how to react, the best advice is to wait and (8) ______ how other people at the table react.
If that doesn't help, be very careful (9) ______ what you say!
I would suggest (10) ______ one solution could be to say, 'That was delicious, but then I knew it would be!'

sections.title: B: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

items.sentence: My dad never has sugar in coffee or tea but he does sometimes like to add artificial (SWEET) ______.
I don't know how anyone could eat eyeballs. That's (DISGUST) ______!
Jenny stood (ANXIOUS) ______ in the kitchen, hoping that her soufflé would rise.
Did you know that tomatoes (ORIGIN) ______ from South America?
Donald is so (CREATE) ______ in the kitchen. I think he should be a professional chef.
There are sandwiches for you in a plastic (CONTAIN) ______ in the fridge.
It's important to observe basic health and (SAFE) ______ precautions when you're handling uncooked meat.
That was (THOROUGH) ______ delicious! Thank you!

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
You have to stir the soup every five minutes or so.
stirred
The soup ______ every five minutes or so.

A baker delivered the cake for us this morning.
had
We ______ a baker this morning.

Elaine tasted the curry for me to see how hot it was.
got
I ______ the curry for me to see how hot it was.

Many people say that olive oil is good for the heart.
said
Olive oil ______ good for the heart.

Some people have said that red wine is good for the heart too.
been
It ______ that red wine is good for the heart too.

They've been building that new supermarket for months.
construction
That new supermarket ______ for months.

These cucumbers came from our garden!
grown
These cucumbers ______ our garden!

Why don't you ask an electrician to fit your cooker?
get
Why don't you ______ an electrician?

I don't think there's enough salt in the sauce.
lacking
I think the sauce ______ salt.
28 I think this milk has gone
items.match: off so let's throw it away.

items.sentence: 29 I ran
items.match: into Bob in the supermarket yesterday.

items.sentence: 30 We've run out
items.match: of bread so could you go and get some?

32 It must be getting
33 We'd love you to come
34 This lasagne hasn't turned
There's a pack of ______ vegetables in the freezer, I think.
There are no vegetarian dishes on the ______!
______ the onions in cooking oil until they're golden brown.
Pre-heat the ______ to 200°.
I'll ______ the washing-up tonight!
The problem with most fizzy drinks is that they're ______ sugar.
Susie and Fran ______ in on us last night, so I had to quickly defrost a pizza.
